# jumble

The objective of jumble is to provide a discrete colour palette that
looks pretty, but is also relatively colourblind safe.

## Installation

You can install the development version of jumble from
[GitHub](https://github.com/) with:

``` r
# install.packages("pak")
pak::pak("davidhodge931/jumble")
```

## Example

jumble provides a 7 colour discrete colour palette.

The first 4 colours are colour-blind safe for deutanomaly, protanomaly
and tritanomaly. The first 5 colours are colour-blind safe for
deutanomaly. The first 3 colours are desaturated safe.

The colours within the palette are provided with accessible names.

``` r
library(ggplot2)
library(jumble)

scales::show_col(jumble)
```

![](reference/figures/README-example-1.png)

# Package index

## All functions

- [`grey`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/jumble/reference/grey.md) :
  grey colour
- [`jumble`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/jumble/reference/jumble.md)
  : A discrete colour palette
- [`navy`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/jumble/reference/navy.md) :
  Navy colour
- [`orange`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/jumble/reference/orange.md)
  : Orange colour
- [`pink`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/jumble/reference/pink.md) :
  Pink colour
- [`red`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/jumble/reference/red.md) : Red
  colour
- [`slate`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/jumble/reference/slate.md) :
  Slate colour
- [`teal`](https://davidhodge931.github.io/jumble/reference/teal.md) :
  Teal colour
